extra.reasoning:
The safety property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED.

Root cause: transition t40 — "on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 12): sent := true". This is the only transition that sets sent:=true while opted_out can be true. The guard requires opted_out AND stage>=12. The maximum stage is 12, and opted_out is independent of stage (the optout event t1 sets opted_out:=true but never modifies stage, and stage transitions t3-t38 never modify opted_out). Therefore a state with opted_out=true and stage=12 is reachable, and a send_attempt there fires t40, producing opted_out=true with sent=true.

Path to stage 12 (the stage-advancing key at each stage): stage0--key_2-->1 (t3), 1--key_1-->2 (t6), 2--key_0-->3 (t9), 3--key_0-->4 (t12), 4--key_2-->5 (t15), 5--key_2-->6 (t18), 6--key_2-->7 (t21), 7--key_2-->8 (t24), 8--key_0-->9 (t27), 9--key_1-->10 (t30), 10--key_0-->11 (t33), 11--key_2-->12 (t36). That is 12 events. Then optout (t1) sets opted_out=true, stage stays 12, sent=false. Then send_attempt: since opted_out and stage>=12, t40 fires setting sent:=true. Final state opted_out=true, sent=true — violation.

Total 14 events, within the 15-event bound. Verified by direct simulation of all 40 transitions with priority-by-order matching: every step matches exactly one enabled transition and the final state is opted_out=true, sent=true.
